tldr: Rekognition is a vision API and react-intl is an i18n library — this is an adoption, not a one-to-one migration.
why_switch: react-intl is free, open source, and the de facto React i18n choice.
Full ICU MessageFormat support for plurals and selects.
Aligns better with localization needs than a vision API.
what_to_export: Hardcoded user-facing strings in React components
Sample components for QA per locale
export_method: Run the FormatJS/react-intl extractor (or a manual grep) over the codebase to collect translatable strings into a base catalog. If translations exist in other systems, export them as JSON keyed by message ID. Consult the react-intl official docs for extractor configuration.
import_method: Install react-intl and the FormatJS CLI, wrap the app in IntlProvider with the active locale and messages, and replace hardcoded strings with FormattedMessage or intl.formatMessage. Add a build step to compile/extract messages and load locale data on demand. Consult react-intl official docs for the exact setup.
gotchas: ICU syntax for plurals/selects has a learning curve.
Locale data must be loaded for each supported locale; lazy-load to control bundle size.
react-intl does not translate — you still need translators or MT.
Server-side rendering requires passing the active locale and messages explicitly.
Not a vision-API replacement — keep Rekognition or pick a vision tool if needed.

email.subject: Migrating from Amazon Rekognition to react-intl
email.body:
Hi team,

Rekognition and react-intl address different problems. Assuming localization is the actual goal, the plan is to install react-intl, wrap the app in IntlProvider, extract strings into per-locale JSON, and replace hardcoded text with FormattedMessage. Estimated effort: a few hours to a couple of days. We'll keep or replace any vision functionality separately.

Thanks
